servinfo.title: Driver Air Bag (Inflator) Module Removal and Installation

attention1.ptxt: Failure to take proper precautions when performing service on the driver air bag (inflator) module can result in personal injury or non-activation of the air bag in the event of a collision.

item.ptxt: Never attempt to disassemble or repair an air bag (inflator) module. If any abnormality is found, replace the module with a new one.

Carefully read Precautions for Air Bag System Service , Precautions for Handling and Storage of Air Bag System Components and Precautions for Disposal of Air Bag and Seat Belt Pretensioner and follow all of the instructions provided.

attention4.ptxt: An air bag (inflator) module that has been dropped may not operate correctly.
Never use an air bag (inflator) module that has been dropped. Replace the module with a new one.

attention3.ptxt: Refer to Repair and Inspection Required after Accident before replacing deployed air bag.

s1.ptxt: Remove driver air bag (inflator) module bolts (2) of driver air bag (inflator) module at both sides of steering wheel (1) using torx (R) wrench (T30).
Disconnect driver air bag (inflator) module connector (2) as shown in figure.
Remove driver air bag (inflator) module from steering wheel.

servinfosub.title: Installation
servinfosub.ptxt: Reverse removal procedure noting the following points.

item.ptxt: Lock connector for driver air bag (inflator) module securely.
Tighten driver air bag (inflator) module bolts to specified torque.
